notes: Seeded from content/cities/edinburgh-board.json as initial published modern Edinburgh snapshot.

payload.name: Edinburgh
payload.summary: A lightweight 8x8 board mapping of Edinburgh neighborhoods and subdistricts, arranged to preserve broad west-east and north-south relationships without chasing perfect geospatial fidelity.

districts.name: Cramond
districts.dayProfile: Walkers, cafes, and village routines cluster around the landward centre.
districts.reviewNotes: Positioned as the village centre rather than the exposed foreshore.
districts.nightProfile: Quiet lanes and the river edge make it feel tucked away after dark.

districts.name: Silverknowes
districts.dayProfile: Open shoreline and suburban movement.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly still once the coast empties.

districts.name: Davidson's Mains
districts.dayProfile: School runs and local errands set the pace.
districts.nightProfile: Pub light and housing streets dominate.

districts.name: Trinity
districts.dayProfile: Steady residential traffic and school activity.
districts.nightProfile: Reserved and dimmer than the city core.

districts.name: Newhaven
districts.dayProfile: Harbour traces and waterfront trade stay visible.
districts.nightProfile: Harbour lights and food spots carry the mood.

districts.name: Leith Shore
districts.dayProfile: Walkers, cafes, and mixed-use blocks keep the waterfront visibly active.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to replace a weaker open-quay placeholder.
districts.nightProfile: Bars and restaurants make evenings busier than nearby residential streets.

districts.name: Leith
districts.dayProfile: Busy, mixed, and commercially active.
districts.reviewNotes: Kept broader than Leith Shore by focusing landmarks on the main district spine.
districts.nightProfile: One of the livelier evening districts.

districts.name: Easter Road
districts.dayProfile: Local shops, buses, and tenement streets keep the district moving.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to replace a low-density shoreline fringe district.
districts.nightProfile: The main corridor stays lit and active after side streets quieten.

districts.name: Barnton
districts.dayProfile: Commuter traffic and suburban routines.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly quiet once the main roads ease.

districts.name: Blackhall
districts.dayProfile: School, errands, and neighborhood loops.
districts.nightProfile: Low-key and domestic.

districts.name: Clermiston
districts.dayProfile: Local retail and bus corridors keep it moving.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter blocks around a few main roads.

districts.name: Inverleith
districts.dayProfile: Walkers, families, and park traffic.
districts.nightProfile: Calmer once the park empties.

districts.name: Stockbridge
districts.dayProfile: Cafes, markets, and polished local traffic.
districts.nightProfile: Social but more contained than rowdy.

districts.name: Bonnington
districts.dayProfile: A quieter connector between bigger districts.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly residential calm.

districts.name: Leith Walk
districts.dayProfile: Buses, shops, takeaways, and constant foot traffic make it one of the city’s busiest connectors.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to represent the denser inner approach into Leith more directly than Pilrig.
districts.nightProfile: Late retail and nightlife spill keep the corridor visibly active after dark.

districts.name: Lochend
districts.dayProfile: Residential activity around the park edge.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter than nearby Leith.

districts.name: Corstorphine
districts.dayProfile: Traffic-heavy but long-established.
districts.nightProfile: Main roads stay busy while side streets settle.

districts.name: Murrayfield
districts.dayProfile: Usually calm outside event traffic.
districts.nightProfile: Event nights change the whole rhythm.

districts.name: Haymarket
districts.dayProfile: Commuters, offices, hotels, and tram traffic keep the district in constant motion.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to strengthen the west-central board with a denser land-based district.
districts.nightProfile: Travel, bars, and late movement make it feel active beyond office hours.

districts.name: West End
districts.dayProfile: Professional footfall and hospitality overlap constantly.
districts.nightProfile: After-work energy keeps it lively.

districts.name: New Town West
districts.dayProfile: Offices, shops, and visitors keep the western half of the New Town busy all day.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to split the broad New Town into more usable board districts.
districts.nightProfile: Lively, polished, and active around bars, hotels, and late buses.

districts.name: New Town East
districts.dayProfile: Retail, offices, and cross-city foot traffic keep the eastern New Town in motion.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to complement New Town West and reduce reliance on a single umbrella district.
districts.nightProfile: Evening activity thins more gradually than in quieter residential streets nearby.

districts.name: Broughton
districts.dayProfile: Dense local movement and independent retail.
districts.nightProfile: Restaurant traffic keeps it lively.

districts.name: Meadowbank
districts.dayProfile: Sports facilities and arterial movement define it.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter once facilities close.

districts.name: Saughton
districts.dayProfile: Parks, schools, and working streets mix together.
districts.nightProfile: Residential and subdued after park traffic fades.

districts.name: Gorgie
districts.dayProfile: Constant road movement and neighborhood trade.
districts.nightProfile: Match nights can shift the mood quickly.

districts.name: Dalry
districts.dayProfile: A busy urban strip with constant movement.
districts.nightProfile: Transit and pubs keep it active.

districts.name: Tollcross
districts.dayProfile: Transit, visitors, and gradual changes to the city core keep the area moving.
districts.nightProfile: After-dark footfall and a bit of friction give it a nervous energy.

districts.name: Old Town
districts.dayProfile: Tourists, historic sites, and crowded thoroughfares make this the city’s showcase quarter.
districts.reviewNotes: Retained as the historic core, with boundaries tighened as surrounding central districts became more specific.
districts.nightProfile: The district keeps its stage-set atmosphere after dark, with bars, ghost tours, and heavy footfall on the Royal Mile.

districts.name: Canongate
districts.dayProfile: Civic buildings, visitors, and historic corridors give it a formal public presence.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to give the eastern foot of the Old Town a more distinct presence.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter than Old Town but still formal in feel with the Parliament edge and the bottom of the Royal Mile.

districts.name: Calton
districts.dayProfile: Vertical transitions and landmarks make it feel different from flatter central districts.
districts.nightProfile: Dramatic vantages, quieter paths, and the hillside create a more atmospheric mood.

districts.name: Holyrood
districts.dayProfile: Parliament, palace, and civic routes make it feel ceremonial rather than everyday.
districts.nightProfile: Broad roads and monumental space give it a more empty, official feeling after dark.

districts.name: Slateford
districts.dayProfile: Roads, rail, and local retail keep it functional.
districts.nightProfile: The transport feel remains after dark.

districts.name: Shandon
districts.dayProfile: The canal softens an otherwise residential district.
districts.nightProfile: Quiet streets and dark water shape the mood.

districts.name: Bruntsfield
districts.dayProfile: Independent shops and local footfall keep it polished.
districts.nightProfile: Evenings stay sociable without turning chaotic.

districts.name: Marchmont
districts.dayProfile: Academic and residential rhythms overlap constantly.
districts.nightProfile: Busy enough to stay active but less loud than nightlife corridors.

districts.name: Southside West
districts.dayProfile: Pedestrian traffic, food spots, and student routines keep the western Southside bright and busy.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to split the broad Southside into more precise board districts.
districts.nightProfile: Late food, bars, and dense foot traffic keep it active well into the evening.

districts.name: Southside East
districts.dayProfile: Main-road movement and mixed residential streets keep the eastern Southside in near-constant use.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to complement Southside West and replace a broader district label.
districts.nightProfile: Restaurants, buses, and student traffic keep it lively after dark.

districts.name: Prestonfield
districts.dayProfile: Green edges and quieter housing slow the pace.
districts.nightProfile: Dark greenspace makes it feel more secluded.

districts.name: Duddingston
districts.dayProfile: Village feel survives beside major parkland.
districts.nightProfile: Atmospheric and quiet with the loch nearby.

districts.name: Wester Hailes
districts.dayProfile: Large housing infrastructure and shopping nodes define it.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter between hubs and road corridors.

districts.name: Craiglockhart
districts.dayProfile: Residential slopes and campus movement shape the day.
districts.nightProfile: Withdrawn and lightly trafficked after dark.

districts.name: Morningside
districts.dayProfile: Shops, schools, and steady local footfall keep it polished.
districts.nightProfile: Calmer after business hours but still social.

districts.name: Grange
districts.dayProfile: Quiet wealth and broad streets shape the pace.
districts.nightProfile: One of the quieter southern districts.

districts.name: Blackford
districts.dayProfile: Residential calm meets scientific landmarks.
districts.nightProfile: The hill makes it feel contemplative after dark.

districts.name: Liberton
districts.dayProfile: A long-settled district with a slower local rhythm.
districts.nightProfile: Quiet housing and wider roads dominate.

districts.name: Craigmillar
districts.dayProfile: Housing and regeneration sites shape the area.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter residential sections beside arterial edges.

districts.name: Portobello
districts.dayProfile: Promenade, high street, and beach draw constant movement.
districts.nightProfile: The shore keeps it active after dark.

districts.name: Currie
districts.dayProfile: Village-suburb routines shape the day.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly quiet and local.

districts.name: Juniper Green
districts.dayProfile: A small linear district tied to the road through it.
districts.nightProfile: The village feel becomes more pronounced after traffic drops.

districts.name: Colinton
districts.dayProfile: Paths and heritage fabric keep it distinctive.
districts.nightProfile: Calm and picturesque in the evening.

districts.name: Fairmilehead
districts.dayProfile: A hinge point between suburb and hillside edge.
districts.nightProfile: Main-road light shapes the evening atmosphere.

districts.name: Buckstone
districts.dayProfile: Family and school rhythms dominate the district.
districts.nightProfile: Quiet cul-de-sacs define the mood.

districts.name: Gilmerton
districts.dayProfile: A practical southern district with local trade.
districts.nightProfile: Main junctions stay active even as side streets quieten.

districts.name: Niddrie
districts.dayProfile: Large housing blocks and main corridors define the pace.
districts.nightProfile: Residential pockets alternate with harder road edges.

districts.name: Joppa
districts.dayProfile: A calmer eastern coastal strip tied to the promenade.
districts.nightProfile: Sea air and low-lit streets create a reflective feel.

districts.name: Balerno
districts.dayProfile: An outer village rhythm with a rural edge nearby.
districts.nightProfile: Feels detached from the city core.

districts.name: Baberton
districts.dayProfile: Suburban and car-oriented with open edges nearby.
districts.nightProfile: Very quiet, with little movement beyond residents.

districts.name: Oxgangs
districts.dayProfile: Steady local movement shaped by buses and schools.
districts.nightProfile: Subdued, with activity near main roads.

districts.name: Kaimes
districts.dayProfile: A quieter southern estate district with a local rhythm.
districts.nightProfile: Dim and low-key.

districts.name: Alnwickhill
districts.dayProfile: A low-density southern edge district.
districts.nightProfile: More road-noise than street-life after dark.

districts.name: Burdiehouse
districts.dayProfile: Housing and edge-of-city infrastructure shape the area.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly residential, with darker fringes.

districts.name: Greendykes
districts.dayProfile: An area shaped by housing renewal and broad roads.
districts.nightProfile: Patchy lighting and newer housing mix together.

districts.name: Brunstane
districts.dayProfile: A quieter eastern edge shaped by rail and housing.
districts.nightProfile: Feels detached from the core after dark.

payload.reviewNotes: Milestone 2 prototype mapping assembled from public neighborhood references and arranged for readable board play rather than full cartographic fidelity. Northern waterfront entries were later revised toward stronger land-based districts and village centres, and dense central umbrella districts were narrowed into more specific corridors where useful.
payload.boardOrientation: Ranks run roughly north to south, files roughly west to east.

review_notes: Milestone 2 prototype mapping assembled from public neighborhood references and arranged for readable board play rather than full cartographic fidelity. districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to add a denser central-west district than the broader redevelopment framing of Fountainbridge.
districts.nightProfile: Restaurants, bars, and cinema traffic make evenings noticeably louder.

districts.name: Old Town North
districts.dayProfile: Visitors, institutions, and steep pedestrian flows keep the northern Old Town packed.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to make the historic core more granular for board play.
districts.nightProfile: Crowded lanes and landmark lighting keep the district animated after dark.

districts.name: Calton
districts.dayProfile: Tourist views and city-edge movement overlap.
districts.nightProfile: The hill becomes more atmospheric after dark.

districts.name: Abbeyhill
districts.dayProfile: Residential but tightly connected to the centre.
districts.nightProfile: Calmer than the core, never fully detached.

districts.name: Willowbrae
districts.dayProfile: Roads and hillside housing shape the district.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter once through-traffic thins.

payload.reviewNotes: Milestone 2 prototype mapping assembled from public neighborhood references and arranged for readable board play rather than full cartographic fidelity. Northern waterfront entries were later revised toward stronger land-based districts and village centres, and dense central umbrella districts were narrowed into more specific corridors where useful.
payload.boardOrientation: Ranks run roughly north to south, files roughly west to east.

review_notes: Milestone 2 prototype mapping assembled from public neighborhood references and arranged for readable board play rather than full cartographic fidelity. Northern waterfront entries were later revised toward stronger land-based districts and village centres, and dense central umbrella districts were narrowed into more specific corridors where useful.

payload.name: Edinburgh
payload.summary: A lightweight 8x8 board mapping of Edinburgh neighborhoods and subdistricts, arranged to preserve broad west-east and north-south relationships without chasing perfect geospatial fidelity.

districts.name: Cramond
districts.dayProfile: Walkers, cafes, and village routines cluster around the landward centre.
districts.reviewNotes: Positioned as the village centre rather than the exposed foreshore.
districts.nightProfile: Quiet lanes and the river edge make it feel tucked away after dark.

districts.name: Silverknowes
districts.dayProfile: Open shoreline and suburban movement.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly still once the coast empties.

districts.name: Davidson's Mains
districts.dayProfile: School runs and local errands set the pace.
districts.nightProfile: Pub light and housing streets dominate.

districts.name: Trinity
districts.dayProfile: Steady residential traffic and school activity.
districts.nightProfile: Reserved and dimmer than the city core.

districts.name: Newhaven
districts.dayProfile: Harbour traces and waterfront trade stay visible.
districts.nightProfile: Harbour lights and food spots carry the mood.

districts.name: Leith Shore
districts.dayProfile: Walkers, cafes, and mixed-use blocks keep the waterfront visibly active.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to replace a weaker open-quay placeholder.
districts.nightProfile: Bars and restaurants make evenings busier than nearby residential streets.

districts.name: Leith
districts.dayProfile: Busy, mixed, and commercially active.
districts.reviewNotes: Kept broader than Leith Shore by focusing landmarks on the main district spine.
districts.nightProfile: One of the livelier evening districts.

districts.name: Easter Road
districts.dayProfile: Local shops, buses, and tenement streets keep the district moving.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to replace a low-density shoreline fringe district.
districts.nightProfile: The main corridor stays lit and active after side streets quieten.

districts.name: Barnton
districts.dayProfile: Commuter traffic and suburban routines.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly quiet once the main roads ease.

districts.name: Blackhall
districts.dayProfile: School, errands, and neighborhood loops.
districts.nightProfile: Low-key and domestic.

districts.name: Clermiston
districts.dayProfile: Local retail and bus corridors keep it moving.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter blocks around a few main roads.

districts.name: Inverleith
districts.dayProfile: Walkers, families, and park traffic.
districts.nightProfile: Calmer once the park empties.

districts.name: Stockbridge
districts.dayProfile: Cafes, markets, and polished local traffic.
districts.nightProfile: Social but more contained than rowdy.

districts.name: Bonnington
districts.dayProfile: A quieter connector between bigger districts.
districts.nightProfile: Mostly residential calm.

districts.name: Leith Walk
districts.dayProfile: Buses, shops, takeaways, and constant foot traffic make it one of the city’s busiest connectors.
districts.reviewNotes: Promoted from planning overlay to represent the denser inner approach into Leith more directly than Pilrig.
districts.nightProfile: Late retail and nightlife spill keep the corridor visibly active after dark.

districts.name: Lochend
districts.dayProfile: Residential activity around the park edge.
districts.nightProfile: Quieter than nearby Leith.

districts.name: Corstorphine
districts.dayProfile: Traffic-heavy but long-established.
districts.nightProfile: Main roads stay busy while side streets settle.

districts.name: Murrayfield
districts.dayProfile: Usually calm outside event traffic.
districts.nightProfile: Event nights change the whole rhythm.

districts.name: Haymarket
